codeReview.summary: Implementation fully covers both inventory valuation and GST return reporting requirements. Inventory valuation returns itemized data with quantity, unit cost, and total value per item; correctly applies period-level costing method (FIFO/LIFO/WEIGHTED_AVERAGE) with fallback to item-level defaults; and supports grouping by category and brand. GST return report aggregates CGST/SGST/IGST output tax from sales invoices and input tax credit from purchase invoices, groups by tax rate, includes transaction-level details, and computes net liability per component. The GstService properly handles intra/inter-state tax splitting. Purchase input tax credit correctly prorates for returned quantities. Frontend handoff documentation is thorough with endpoint maps, user flows, data contracts, and error code updates. All targeted tests (19/19) and gate-fast baseline (380/380) passed.

issues.description: ReportService is now 1247 lines, significantly exceeding the 500-line convention. The GST return logic (~380 new lines including gstReturn(), resolveRequestedPeriod(), resolveInvoiceTaxBreakdown(), resolvePurchaseTaxBreakdown(), and supporting methods) was added directly to this already-large service. The established pattern in this module is to delegate to focused query services (TrialBalanceReportQueryService, ProfitLossReportQueryService, BalanceSheetReportQueryService, AgedDebtorsReportQueryService). The GST return logic should ideally be extracted to a GstReturnReportQueryService following this same pattern.

InventoryValuationService is now 516 lines, marginally over the 500-line convention. The additions for period-aware costing, item snapshots, and production product mapping contributed to the growth. Minor and could be addressed in a future cleanup pass.

Asymmetry in status filtering: isIncludedInvoiceStatus() excludes DRAFT status for sales invoices, but isIncludedPurchaseStatus() does NOT exclude DRAFT for purchase invoices. This may be intentional if purchase invoices don't use a DRAFT state, but the asymmetry should be verified against the domain model to ensure draft purchases aren't inadvertently included in GST return calculations.

GstRateAccumulator.taxableAmount accumulates from both output (sales) and input (purchase) directions into a single field, reported as a combined taxableAmount in GstRateSummary. For a GST return filing worksheet, it may be more useful to separate output taxable base from input taxable base. The individual component splits (outputCgst/outputSgst/outputIgst, inputCgst/inputSgst/inputIgst) are correctly separated.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker added ~380 lines of GST return logic directly to ReportService (now 1247 lines) instead of following the established pattern of extracting to a dedicated query service (like TrialBalanceReportQueryService, ProfitLossReportQueryService, etc.). AGENTS.md states 'No service class should exceed ~500 lines' and 'Single responsibility' but does not explicitly require the ReportQueryService delegation pattern. The pattern is evident from existing code but not documented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ReportService.java is 1247 lines. Compare to existing delegated services: TrialBalanceReportQueryService, ProfitLossReportQueryService, BalanceSheetReportQueryService, AgedDebtorsReportQueryService are all separate focused services that ReportService delegates to.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed the skill procedure correctly: read feature description and AGENTS.md, wrote tests, implemented, verified with targeted and gate-fast suites, and updated frontend handoff. skillFeedback confirms followedProcedure=true with no deviations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback: { followedProcedure: true, deviations: [], suggestedChanges: [] }. Verification ran compile, targeted tests (19 pass), gate-fast (380 pass), and full suite (pre-existing failures noted as out of scope).

summary: PASS. Feature 'inventory-valuation-and-gst-reports' fully implements both required reports. Inventory valuation returns item-level data with period-aware costing (FIFO/LIFO/WAC) and category/brand grouping. GST return report aggregates output/input tax by component (CGST/SGST/IGST), groups by tax rate, includes transaction details, and computes net liability. Both endpoints are documented in frontend-handoff.md with data contracts and user flows. All 4 non-blocking issues relate to code organization (ReportService size, InventoryValuationService size) and minor design observations (status filtering asymmetry, combined taxable amount). No blocking issues found.
